raw_cv_text:
[CANDIDATE_NAME]        
  Winnipeg, MB, Canada 
[EMAIL]                                                                Phone: [PHONE] 
https://www.linkedin.com/in/prabu-karthick-parameshwar 

SUMMARY 
 Highly-driven  researcher  with  7+  years  of  experience  in  cell  culture,  biomaterials,  microfabrication  and 

tissue engineering as well as strong leadership and cross-disciplinary research expertise  
 Looking to join a biotech company to contribute towards research efforts (willing to relocate) 

EDUCATION 
o  Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.), Biological and Biomedical Engineering  
Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences, McGill University, Montréal, QC, Canada 
  Fast-tracked from Master of Engineering (M. Eng – Thesis Based) in 2019 
  Thesis: Microenvironmental regulation of placental trophoblast fusion 

(cGPA: 3.95/4.00)    2017-2024 

o  Bachelor of Applied Science (B.A.Sc.) in Engineering Science  
Faculty of Applied Science and Engineering, University of Toronto, Toronto, ON, Canada 

(cGPA: 3.27/4.00)    2013-2017 

  Major: Biomedical Systems Engineering 
  Thesis: Mechanical stimulation of cell-seeded hydrogels for connective tissue engineering. 

SKILLS & EXPERTISE 
•  Technical Skills: human cell culture (cell lines, primary cells, stem cells, co-culture), differentiation, histology, 

microscopy (fluorescence, confocal), microfabrication, microfluidics, instrumentation, 3D (bio)printing  
•  Bioassays: RNA/DNA extraction, PCR, gel electrophoresis, UV spectrometry, immunostaining, ELISA 
•  Software: MS Office, AutoCAD, LabVIEW, ImageJ, Biorender, MATLAB, G-code, Statistics – JASP, JMP 
•  Lab Certifications: Biosafety, WHMIS 2015, Hazardous waste management, up-to-date vaccinations 
•  Soft skills: Organization, time management, critical thinking, resilience and adaptability 

RESEARCH EXPERIENCE 
•  Graduate Research Scientist @ McGill University 

   Sept 2017-April 2024 
o  Worked  with  Dr.  [CANDIDATE_NAME]  on  in  vitro  differentiation  of  placenta  trophoblast  cells  to  create 

fused sheets through biomechanical and biomaterial interventional methods  

o  Designed and implemented a process to fabricate  nanoparticles from decellularized placentas, as  well  as 
characterized its efficacy in using it as a fusion-inducing biomaterial coating on hydrogels with cell sheets 
o  Collaborated with Dr. [CANDIDATE_NAME] at the Montréal General Hospital to evaluate mechanical 
compression protocols and enhanced pro-fusion conditions by up to 30%  in 2D cell sheets/3D spheroids 

o  Modified an existing 3D printer to create a moving UV light source for lithography applications 
o  Developed and established an SOP for the fabrication and utilization of a microfluidic device for extended 

culture of testes tissues over 14 days to assess spermatogenesis in a rat model 
  Done  in  collaboration  with  Dr.  [CANDIDATE_NAME]  Nagano  at  the  Research  Institute  of  the  [CANDIDATE_NAME]  University 

Health Centre (RI-MUHC) from 2019-2022; eventually to be used for human experiments 

o  Lead the planning, experimental design and execution of 7 multidisciplinary projects along with supervis-
ing  5  undergraduate  research  students, resulting  in 5  conference  presentations,  4  journal  articles,  1  tech-
nical note and 2 literature review publications (Google Scholar) 

•  Undergraduate Research Scientist @ University of Toronto   

   May 2016-April 2017 
o  Worked  with  Dr.  [CANDIDATE_NAME]  to  implement  high-frequency  mechanical  stimulation  on  hydrogel-
encapsulated mesenchymal stem cells and maximally promoted differentiation into myofibroblasts and their 
protein production over 7 days 

o  Created a basic experimental setup and developed a computer interface to perform electrical cell impedance 

sensing (ECIS) over several weeks in a blood-brain barrier model 

1 

        
 
 
 
 
 
 
    
 
 
    
 [CANDIDATE_NAME]        
  Winnipeg, MB, Canada 
[EMAIL]                                                                Phone: [PHONE] 
https://www.linkedin.com/in/prabu-karthick-parameshwar 

LEADERSHIP EXPERIENCE 
•  Cleanroom Manager and Safety Officer @ [CANDIDATE_NAME], McGill University    

       Jan 2019-Dec 2023 

o  Co-ordinated  the  establishment  of  new  cleanroom  workspace  in  the  lab  from  room  expansion  to                

installation  of  a  portable  cleanroom  tent  along  with  the  set  up  of  microfabrication  equipment  such  as  a 
work hood, spincoater, 3D printer, degassing chambers, ovens and UV protective lights 

o  Trained and helped 30+ users get started with their projects on various aspects of microfabrication 
o  Established  contacts  with  industry  sales  and  technical  representatives  to  arrange  for  quotations,  products 

and expertise to perform scheduled maintenance on a plasma cleaner and vacuum pumps 

o  [CANDIDATE_NAME] up-to-date records of cleanroom product inventory and a users database for valid certifications  
o  Ensured the safe operation of this facility by organizing regular safety tours, developed and graded safety 

quizzes to assess levels of understanding and updated protocols as required 

o  Contributed to the development of organ-on-chip models for the placenta, salivary glands and gut 

TEACHING EXPERIENCE 
•  Teaching Assistant  @ Department of Bioengineering, McGill University                 

  BIEN 290 (Bioengineering Measurement Laboratory)  

       Sept-Dec 2019, 2021, 2022 & 2023 
  Undergraduate level course which deals with lab safety and basic hands-on wet lab concepts (including 
3D bioprinting with collagen) for introductory bioengineering experiments (200+ students over 4 years) 

  Lead laboratory sessions, demonstrated experiments and assessed lab reports; 180 hours per term 
  Delivered a guest lecture on MATLAB for simulation analysis (2021-2022) 
  Designed a new lab module on using Design of Experiments (DoE) for bead size analysis (2023) 

  BIEN 530 (Imaging and Bioanalytical Instrumentation) 

  Jan-Apr 2021 

  Graduate/Undergraduate level course which provides an overview of several microscopy systems and 

image analysis techniques using ImageJ (30 students) 

  Assessed problem sets, midterms and partially graded final projects; 85 hours per term 

CERTIFICATIONS/ADDITIONAL TRAINING 
•  Regulatory -  What is CMC, QbD, QA/QC and facility qualification 
•  Introduction to Cell Therapy and principles of cell line development  
•  Regulatory – What is GMP, GDP, [CANDIDATE_NAME] and process validation   
•  On-Demand Training Courses

LANGUAGES KNOWN

EXTRA-CURRICULARS 
•  Graduate Orientation Ambassador  @ Campus Life & Engagement, McGill University  

        2018-2023 
o  Introduced various University and student-led services available for new graduate students as the graduate 

student ambassador for my department’s student orientations every year since 2018 

INTERESTS & HOBBIES 
•  Museums, drums, photography, cooking

pitch_text:
[EXCEL SOURCE]: 1. R&D Engineer/ Scientist​
2. Process engineer​
3. Application Scientist​
4. Clinical Researcher​
5. R&D Manager​ 


[FILE: clarify_Parameshwar.pdf]
Document 1: Assignment 1 (Clarify) 

Question #1: My Ikigai 

My Ikigai is building the infrastructure for biological discovery. 

The central theme of my career is "Enabling Innovation." I don't just study biology; I engineer the 
environments where it thrives—whether that's designing a microfluidic device to keep rat testes 
tissue alive for 14 days or physically building a cleanroom from scratch at [CANDIDATE_NAME]. My talent lies 
in this intersection of wet-lab biology (placental cells, stem cells) and hard engineering 
(microfabrication, 3D printing). 

The world needs robust, scalable platforms for tissue engineering and regenerative medicine. 
By combining my hands-on "maker" mindset with my rigorous scientific training, I can create a 
lasting impact: developing the tools and processes that turn fragile cells into functional 
therapies. 

To align my career with this, I am actively targeting Biotech R&D and Process Engineering 
roles. I am ready to apply the resilience I developed during my personal challenges to the 
fast-paced, problem-solving environment of industry, moving from "academic publication" to 
"product realization." 

Question #2: My Ideal Day 

My ideal day is a dynamic mix of hands-on fabrication and experimental execution. 

It starts in the cleanroom (or a similar controlled environment), where I am prototyping a new 
microfluidic chip design or calibrating a 3D bioprinter. I love the focus required to tweak a 
G-code script or align a UV light source—skills I honed by modifying equipment during my PhD. 

By mid-day, I transition to the tissue culture hood. I am seeding cells into the device I just built, 
testing a new biomaterial coating derived from decellularized tissue. The afternoon involves 
data analysis—using MATLAB or JMP to statistically validate the results of a Design of 
Experiments (DoE) run. The day ends with a team huddle, where I might troubleshoot a 
colleague's experimental setup, leveraging my experience as a Cleanroom Manager to ensure 
everyone is working safely and efficiently. 

Question #3: Job Titles 

1.  R&D Scientist (Tissue Engineering/Biomaterials): This is my top choice. It leverages 
my PhD work on placental models and my expertise in hydrogels and decellularized 
scaffolds. 

2.  Process Engineer (Biotech/Manufacturing): I enjoy the "how" of science—optimizing 

workflows, establishing SOPs, and ensuring quality (QbD/GMP). My experience 
managing a cleanroom makes me a natural fit here. 

 3.  Application Scientist: I have a strong teaching background (TA for 4 years) and enjoy 

helping others use complex tools (microscopes, printers). I would thrive in a 
customer-facing technical role. 

4.  Microfluidics Engineer: My specific experience designing chips for organ-on-a-chip 

models (testes, placenta) is a highly specialized and valuable skill set for companies like 
Aspect Biosystems or precision medicine startups. 

Question #4: S.M.A.R.T Goals 

●  Goal 1 (Career Placement): Secure a Scientist or Engineer role in a Canadian biotech 

company (Target: Aspect Biosystems, BlueRock, or STEMCELL) by July 2026. 

○  Measurable: Apply to 5 roles weekly; network with 2 employees from my target 

list per month. 

○  Attainable: I have already had interviews with similar companies; I need to refine 

my closing interview performance. 

●  Goal 2 (Skill Certification): Complete the "Regulatory Affairs for Biologics" certification 

to solidify my knowledge of GMP/CMC by May 2026. 

○  Measurable: Certificate obtained. 
○  Realistic: Builds on the Cytiva courses I completed in 2024/2025. 

●  Goal 3 (Professional Networking): Re-engage with the scientific community after my 
personal gap by attending 2 local biotech networking events (virtual or in-person) in the 
Winnipeg or Montreal area by August 2026. 

○  Measurable: attendance and follow-up with 3 new contacts per event. 
○  Time-bound: Next 6 months. 
○ 

 
 
 

[FILE: pitch_Parameshwar.pdf]
Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch) 

Selected Job Offer Position: R&D Scientist, Tissue Therapeutics Target: Aspect 
Biosystems or BlueRock Therapeutics Key Requirements: 

●  PhD in Biomedical Engineering or related field. 
●  Experience with 3D bioprinting, microfluidics, and cell culture. 
●  Hands-on experience with equipment maintenance and modification. 
●  Knowledge of GMP and Design of Experiments (DoE). 

The Elevator Pitch 

"Hi, I’m Prabu. I am a Biomedical Engineer who builds the tools that make tissue engineering 
possible. 

During my PhD at [CANDIDATE_NAME], I didn't just use commercial equipment; I modified 3D printers to 
create custom lithography systems and built a microfabrication cleanroom from the ground up. I 
used these tools to engineer complex placental and testicular tissue models, keeping cells alive 
and functional for weeks in microfluidic devices I designed myself. 

I know that in a company like yours, you need people who can troubleshoot the hardware and 
the biology. Because I’ve managed a cleanroom facility and trained over 30 users, I bring a 
unique 'process mindset' to R&D. I’ve recently deepened this with training in GMP and Design 
of Experiments (DoE) to ensure my innovations are scalable. 

I’m looking to bring this blend of engineering grit and biological expertise to your team to help 
advance your next generation of cell therapies."

hard_skills_analysis.skill_name: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales

culture_analysis.dissonance_analysis: Les valeurs de l'entreprise telles que l'innovation, l'excellence opérationnelle et le développement du personnel sont en accord avec les motivations et les compétences du candidat. Cependant, il existe un risque de friction en ce qui concerne la créativité et la liberté d'action, car le candidat cherche à appliquer ses compétences de manière innovante, tandis que l'entreprise peut avoir des processus et des procédures établis.
culture_analysis.retention_risk: RISQUE MOYEN : Le candidat est motivé par la possibilité de créer des outils et des processus innovants, mais il peut être frustré si l'entreprise ne lui offre pas suffisamment de liberté d'action et de créativité. Il est important pour l'entreprise de trouver un équilibre entre la créativité et les processus établis pour maintenir la motivation et l'engagement du candidat.

flags: ℹ️ [INFO] LOCALISATION : Information non fournie par le candidat.

rhetoric_analysis.analysis_text: La structure de la présentation est claire et bien organisée, avec une introduction qui présente le thème central de la carrière du candidat, "Enabling Innovation". Le candidat utilise des exemples concrets pour illustrer ses compétences et ses expériences, ce qui renforce la crédibilité de son discours. Cependant, certains paragraphes sont un peu longs et pourraient être condensés pour améliorer la clarté. Le vocabulaire est généralement précis, mais certains termes techniques pourraient être expliqués pour les non-spécialistes. Le candidat montre une bonne confiance dans ses capacités et une vision claire de ses objectifs, ce qui est très persuasif.

readiness_diagnostic.cot_reasoning: Le candidat présente des forces en termes de motivation et de créativité, mais il existe des faiblesses importantes en termes de compétences techniques. Les valeurs de l'entreprise sont en accord avec les motivations du candidat, mais il existe un risque de friction en ce qui concerne la créativité et la liberté d'action. Les risques logistiques liés au visa et à la localisation sont également à prendre en compte.
hard_skills_gap: Diplôme universitaire en chimie, biochimie ou immunologie
Expérience dans les domaines médical et/ou pharmaceutique et/ou de la cytométrie en flux
Connaissance des procédures opérationnelles standard (SOP) générales
readiness_diagnostic.risk_assessment: Le risque de rétention est moyen, car le candidat est motivé par la possibilité de créer des outils et des processus innovants, mais il peut être frustré si l'entreprise ne lui offre pas suffisamment de liberté d'action et de créativité. Les risques logistiques liés au visa et à la localisation sont également à prendre en compte.
